title: The Tallit: More Than Just a Prayer Shawl
title_zh: 祷告披肩塔利特：不仅仅是一块布
description: History, laws, and customs of the tallit — when to wear it, how to put it on, and the significance of the blue thread.
chinese_dynasty_context: Late Shang Dynasty (商朝晚期, ~1300-1260 BCE)
header_image_description: A serene Chinese shan shui landscape painting in traditional ink wash style, depicting a peaceful morning scene with misty mountains in the background. In the foreground, a flowing white cloth with delicate blue threads draped over a weathered stone, symbolizing the sacred connection between earth and heaven. Soft morning light filters through bamboo trees, creating gentle shadows that suggest spiritual contemplation. The composition uses muted earth tones - grey mountains, white mist, and subtle blue accents - evoking a sense of reverence and timeless tradition. The scene conveys quiet dignity and the harmony between the physical and spiritual worlds.
